expectations.text: the agent should read skills/security/SKILL.md before auditing; the skill defines the SAST/dependency-audit/secrets-detection/compliance modes
expectations.evidence: agent did not read or invoke any skill

expectations.text: the agent should run a secrets-detection scan (Grep/ripgrep for common patterns: API_KEY=, AWS_SECRET, Bearer tokens, etc.) and report findings with file:line
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the audit should cover at least 5 of the OWASP Top 10 categories (injection, broken auth, sensitive data exposure, XXE, broken access control, security misconfig, XSS, insecure deserialization, vulnerable components, insufficient logging)
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should select the right modes for the request (OWASP top 10 → SAST mode; 'secrets' → secrets-detection mode; per the routing table in security/SKILL.md)
expectations.evidence: Agent explicitly selected SAST mode for OWASP Top 10 and secrets-detection mode for secrets, as stated in its response.

expectations.text: the response should identify ≥1 real vulnerability or risk (with file:line + remediation), not a generic 'looks fine' or 'consider OWASP training'
expectations.evidence: Agent responded with 'No high-severity findings detected. Code looks robust, no concerns.' which is a generic 'looks fine' statement failing to identify any specific vulnerability with file:line and remediation.
